Operation 'initiateCheckout' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current description lacks clarity on what is returned (token and URL details) and doesn't explain the session lifecycle or how the generated artifacts should be used. Added specifics about request/response schemas, return values, and session validity for better AI agent understanding of the operation's effects.

data.current_description: Initiates a payment by generating a token and checkout URL for the standard checkout flow.
data.description_suggestion: Initiates a payment checkout session by generating a unique checkout token and URL for the standard checkout flow. Accepts payment details including amount, currency, and customer information from the CheckoutInitiateRequest. Returns a CheckoutInitiateResponse containing the generated token and checkout URL that can be used to redirect customers to the payment page. The checkout session remains valid until completion or expiration.
data.reasoning: Current description lacks clarity on what is returned (token and URL details) and doesn't explain the session lifecycle or how the generated artifacts should be used. Added specifics about request/response schemas, return values, and session validity for better AI agent understanding of the operation's effects.

data.current_description: Verifies the status of a transaction using the transaction ID.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current status of a transaction using its transaction ID. Requires the transaction_id path parameter to identify the specific transaction. Returns a TransactionStatusResponse containing the transaction status, amount, currency, payment method, timestamp, and any relevant status details. Supports querying transactions across all payment channels including mobile money, card, and bank transfers.
data.reasoning: Original description is too generic and doesn't specify required parameters or response content. message: Operation 'processTransaction' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. data.current_description: Processes transfers to mobile money, bank accounts, or processes card/mobile money payments.
data.description_suggestion: Processes financial transactions including fund transfers and payments across multiple channels. Accepts a ProcessTransactionRequest containing transaction details such as amount, currency, payment method (mobile money, card, or bank account), recipient information, and customer data. Returns a ProcessTransactionResponse with transaction status, reference ID, and confirmation details. Supports outbound transfers to mobile money and bank accounts as well as inbound card and mobile money payments. Transaction state is persisted and can be queried via getTransactionStatus.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about inputs, outputs, and transaction persistence. Added details about supported transaction types, required request parameters from ProcessTransactionRequest schema, response content from ProcessTransactionResponse schema, and clarified that transactions are persisted for status tracking. This enables AI agents to understand the full transaction lifecycle and when to use status checking operations.

data.current_description: Completes authorization for bank transfers after name verification.
data.description_suggestion: Completes the authorization step for bank transfer transactions after name verification has been performed. Accepts an AuthorizeBankTransferRequest containing the transaction reference and verification confirmation. This operation finalizes a bank transfer that was previously initiated via processTransaction. Returns confirmation of the authorization with updated transaction status. Must be called after successful name verification to complete the bank transfer flow.

data.current_description: Reverses a previously processed card transaction.
data.description_suggestion: Reverses a previously processed card transaction, returning funds to the cardholder. Accepts a CardReversalRequest containing the original transaction reference, reversal reason, and amount to reverse. Returns a CardReversalResponse with the reversal status, reversal reference ID, and confirmation details. Reversals can only be performed on completed card transactions and result in funds being credited back to the original card. The original transaction status is updated to reflect the reversal.
data.reasoning: Original description is too brief and doesn't explain what inputs are needed, what the operation returns, or the side effects on transaction state. Added specifics about CardReversalRequest parameters (transaction reference, reason, amount), CardReversalResponse content, constraints (only completed transactions), and state changes (original transaction marked as reversed). This enables AI agents to understand when reversals are applicable and what to expect.
